div.p: Alternative to ReadIndex: append no-op entry, wait for commit and apply, then serve read Trade-off: Simpler implementation, higher latency (full replication vs heartbeat quorum)

text.tspan: // Step 2: Create and append no-op entry
// Step 4: Wait for no-op to be applied (with timeout)
// Step 2: Create and append no-op entry
// Step 4: Wait for no-op to be applied (with timeout)

div.p: Latency: ~2-3 RTT (Replication + Commit + Apply) Guarantee: Once no-op applied, leader is confirmed and all prior entries are committed
Once the no-op entry is committed:

ol.li: Leader Confirmed: Only the legitimate leader could have committed an entry in term T+1 (requires majority acknowledgment)
No Stale Leader: A partitioned leader cannot commit, so ReadIndex timeout would catch this
Prior Entries Sealed: By Figure 8 rule, committing the no-op transitively commits all entries before it
Read Sees All Writes: Any write acknowledged before this read started is now committed and will be visible

div.p: Figure 8 Connection: The no-op entry from the current term serves as the "seal" that makes all previous entries (from any term) safely committed.
Complexity: Higher (track pending reads, quorum confirmation)
Guarantee: Leadership confirmed via heartbeat acknowledgment
Guarantee: Entry committed transitively commits all prior
